title: OT Protocol Usage Analysis and Inventory
app: `Cisco Cyber Vision Splunk Add-On` (Splunkbase 5748) · `Nozomi Networks Universal Add-on` (Splunkbase 6905)
spl:
index=nozomi sourcetype="nozomi:link"
| stats dc(src_ip) as sources, dc(dst_ip) as destinations, sum(bps_out) as total_bps by protocols, zone
| sort -sources
| table zone, protocols, sources, destinations, total_bps
description: OT security platforms identify all industrial protocols in use across the OT network — Modbus, EtherNet/IP, Profinet, S7, BACnet, DNP3, IEC 104, OPC-UA, and dozens more. Understanding protocol distribution helps prioritize security investments, plan protocol-specific IDS rules, and identify legacy protocols that need migration.
value: OT security platforms identify all industrial protocols in use across the OT network — Modbus, EtherNet/IP, Profinet, S7, BACnet, DNP3, IEC 104, OPC-UA, and dozens more. Understanding protocol distribution helps prioritize security investments, plan protocol-specific IDS rules, and identify legacy protocols that need migration.
implementation: Both platforms identify all industrial protocols in use via DPI. Analyze data to build protocol inventory per site/zone. Identify unexpected protocols (e.g., BACnet in a power substation, or Modbus in an enterprise zone). Compare protocol usage across sites for standardization. Feed into protocol-specific security policy development.
visualization: Protocol distribution pie chart per site; protocol comparison across sites; unexpected protocol alert table; protocol trend over time.
knownFalsePositives: Protocol mix changes when new skids arrive, fieldbuses are retired, or vendors add diagnostic channels.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Cisco Cyber Vision Splunk Add-On` (Splunkbase 5748) · `Nozomi Networks Universal Add-on` (Splunkbase 6905).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=cisco:cybervision:activity` · Nozomi: `sourcetype=nozomi:link`, `sourcetype=nozomi:session`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Both platforms identify all industrial protocols in use via DPI. Analyze data to build protocol inventory per site/zone. Identify unexpected protocols (e.g., BACnet in a power substation, or Modbus in an enterprise zone). Compare protocol usage across sites for standardization. Feed into protocol-specific security policy development.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=nozomi sourcetype="nozomi:link"
| stats dc(src_ip) as sources, dc(dst_ip) as destinations, sum(bps_out) as total_bps by protocols, zone
| sort -sources
| table zone, protocols, sources, destinations, total_bps
```

Understanding this SPL

**OT Protocol Usage Analysis and Inventory** — OT security platforms identify all industrial protocols in use across the OT network — Modbus, EtherNet/IP, Profinet, S7, BACnet, DNP3, IEC 104, OPC-UA, and dozens more. Understanding protocol distribution helps prioritize security investments, plan protocol-specific IDS rules, and identify legacy protocols that need migration.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=cisco:cybervision:activity` · Nozomi: `sourcetype=nozomi:link`, `sourcetype=nozomi:session`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Cisco Cyber Vision Splunk Add-On` (Splunkbase 5748) · `Nozomi Networks Universal Add-on` (Splunkbase 6905). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: nozomi; **sourcetype**: nozomi:link. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=nozomi, sourcetype="nozomi:link". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by protocols, zone** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Pipeline stage (see **OT Protocol Usage Analysis and Inventory**): table zone, protocols, sources, destinations, total_bps

Step 3 — Validate
Run the search for a time range that should include both routine and change activity, then compare the same assets, flows, and alerts in the Cisco Cyber Vision UI and in Nozomi Guardian or CMC, matching whichever source the event came from. Reconcile severities, counts, and key fields in both tools.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Protocol distribution pie chart per site; protocol comparison across sites; unexpected protocol alert table; protocol trend over time.
grandmaExplanation: We build a view of which industrial protocols are in use and where so unexpected protocols or sites stand out in governance.
